name: IRC_CAND_EMAIL_RECIPIENTS
description: This table stores the recipients of an email that is sent or received.

columns.Name: PERSON_ID
columns.Comments: Foreign key into PER_PERSONS. PersonId for the candidate or recruiter/ hiring manager that is the recipient of this message as part of the interaction.

columns.Name: RECIPIENT_TYPE
columns.Comments: Indicates whether the recipient is in the TO, CC, or BCC list. Possible values are TO, CC, BCC.

columns.Name: RELAYED_FLAG
columns.Comments: Indicate whether this recipient was relayed the message, instead of being part of the recipients list on the original message. Important to display to whom the message was forwarded/relayed to when the conversation participant was not part of the senders/ recipients list.

columns.Name: RELAYED_MESSAGE_IDENTIFIER
columns.Comments: The RelayedMessageIdentifier denotes a unique Id that is sent along with the relayed message. This is populated for every relayed email message with the SMTP MessageId.

columns.Name: RELAYED_TIME
columns.Comments: Indicate the time that the message was relayed.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.
